name: Claude Opus 4.5
note: Efficiency-focused flagship: 67% price cut vs Opus 4.1 and state-of-the-art 80.9% SWE-bench Verified at launch.

results.notes: AIME 2025 without code tools; Anthropic flagged that decontamination efforts for AIME were not fully successful

Terminal-Bench 2.0, per the side-by-side Opus 4.6-vs-4.5 comparison table (confirmed by direct fetch). A separate, unversioned 'Terminal-Bench' figure of 59.3% also circulates in some vendor-relayed writeups (e.g. huggingface.co/blog/Laser585/claude-4-benchmarks) for the same model; kept the versioned 2.0 figure since it is directly comparable to the terminal-bench-2 entries already recorded for Opus 4.6/4.7/4.8.
ARC Prize's own verified run: 'the top verified commercial model, Opus 4.5 (Thinking, 64k), scores 37.6% for $2.20/task' — confirmed via direct fetch.

Anthropic's system card reports this as OSWorld-Verified (P@1, avg@5, 66.26%); some vendor-relayed tables abbreviate the label to plain 'OSWorld', but the score (66.3%) is far above the original OSWorld benchmark's typical range, confirming it is the Verified variant.
Standard single-agent BrowseComp figure from Anthropic's system card, distinct from the higher 'BrowseComp-Plus' fetch-enabled figure (85.3%) Anthropic also reports separately.
Cross-confirmed at 0.982 on the independent llm-stats.com Tau2-Telecom leaderboard (https://llm-stats.com/benchmarks/tau2-telecom).
GDPval-AA Elo, confirmed via direct fetch of the Opus 4.6-vs-4.5 comparison table.
10.6-point improvement over Sonnet 4.5 per Anthropic's own launch post; absolute figure (89.4%) confirmed via direct fetch of this third-party writeup relaying the official number.
No-tools configuration; with-tools scored 73.9% per the same source.

ARC-AGI-1, private/semi-private validation set, 64k thinking budget, per Section 2.10 of the official Claude Opus 4.5 System Card ('The ARC Prize Foundation reports that Claude Opus 4.5 achieved 80.0% on ARC-AGI-1... on their private dataset. This is SOTA for both benchmarks'). Companion to the already-tracked ARC-AGI-2 score (37.6%) from the same evaluation. Independently corroborated via arcprize.org coverage.

SWE-bench Pro (Scale AI's harder 1,865-problem variant), no-thinking configuration, Table 2.4.A of the official System Card. With a 64k thinking budget the score is 51.60% instead. Distinct from the already-tracked plain SWE-bench (Verified) score of 80.9%.
CyberGym, pass@1 aggregate over the 1,505-task suite (averaged across 5 independent replicas), Section 2.14 of the official System Card. No-thinking configuration, 200k context.

Cross-confirmed against claude-opus-4-5-high.json's identical 83.7 livecodebench entry (also Vals.ai-sourced). Non-thinking Vals.ai variant scores lower, 75.03% (https://www.vals.ai/models/anthropic_claude-opus-4-5-20251101), not used here since it doesn't match this file's high-effort headline configuration.
HELM (source type 1) and arXiv/Semantic Scholar (source type 2) were checked first and came up empty for humaneval before this GitHub source resolved it.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real, released model via anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-5: release date 2025-11-24, pricing $5/$25 per million input/output tokens (67% cut vs Opus 4.1), 200K context, SWE-bench Verified 80.9% all literally appear on the official page. Cross-checked all vellum.ai-sourced rows by re-fetching that page directly: GPQA Diamond 87.0%, MMLU 90.8%, MMMU 80.7%, HLE (with search) ~43.2% all literally present. Confirmed AIME 93% (without code tools) and the AIME decontamination caveat by fetching the LessWrong post directly ('93% without code and 100% with Python'; Anthropic disclosed 'rephrased versions of at least some AIME questions... persisted in the training corpus'). Confirmed Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index score of 70 and #2 rank (behind Gemini 3 Pro at 73, tied GPT-5.1) by fetching artificialanalysis.ai directly. No changes to values were needed; architecture/params remain correctly marked null/undisclosed since Anthropic does not publish these.

2026-07-16: Researched the three missing core benchmarks (math, humaneval, livecodebench). MATH-500 and HumanEval were checked against (1) Anthropic's own official launch page (anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-5) and the full Claude Opus 4.5 System Card PDF — fetched directly and full-text-searched; neither benchmark name appears anywhere in either document — and (2) Vals.ai's independent eval suite for this model, whose raw HTML was fetched directly for both the Thinking and Nonthinking variants; Vals tests GPQA Diamond, MMLU Pro, LiveCodeBench, IOI, MMMU, SWE-bench and several enterprise verticals for Opus 4.5, but does not include MATH-500 or HumanEval at all. A sibling cross-check across every other Claude-4-generation corpus file (Opus 4, 4.1, 4.6, 4.7, 4.8, Sonnet 4, 4.5, 4.6, Haiku 4.5) found zero 'math' or 'humaneval' entries anywhere in the family, versus every pre-Claude-4 model file carrying both — a consistent, clean generational cutoff indicating Anthropic and its independent trackers stopped publishing these saturated legacy benchmarks starting with Claude 4, not a research gap specific to Opus 4.5. Both are recorded as omitted_no_evidence. LiveCodeBench, by contrast, is actively tracked by Vals.ai for this model: added at 83.7% (Thinking/high-effort configuration, matching every other headline score already in this file), extracted from the model page's raw HTML (the rendered/JS-summarized view returns non-functional 0.0% placeholders) and cross-confirmed against the identical figure already recorded in the sibling 'Claude Opus 4.5 (High)' corpus entry.

2026-07-17: Second-pass deep research targeting the still-missing 'math' (MATH-500) and 'humaneval' gaps, restricted this round to source types the prior pass had NOT used (official launch page/system card, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena and BenchLM were already exhausted on 2026-07-16). Checked, in order:

(1) Stanford HELM — fetched config.js + the underlying runs_to_run_suites.json/groups.json data files (hosted on storage.googleapis.com/crfm-helm-public) directly for all four live HELM projects: MMLU (release v1.13.0), Lite (v1.13.0), Classic (v1.13.0) and Capabilities (v1.15.0, confirmed the latest release via the GCS bucket listing). Parsed every Anthropic/Claude model identifier out of each dataset: MMLU tops out at Claude 3.5 (2.1 through 3-5-sonnet-20241022); Capabilities goes up to Claude Opus 4, Sonnet 4.5 and Haiku 4.5 but has NOT added Opus 4.5. Claude Opus 4.5 is absent from every HELM project — ruled out entirely.

(2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar — Semantic Scholar's API rate-limited every attempt (429s), so queried arXiv's own API directly for abstracts combining "Claude Opus 4.5" with MMLU (0 hits) and with HumanEval (1 hit: arXiv:2605.09023, "Using Semantic Distance to Estimate Uncertainty in LLM-Based Code Generation"). Downloaded and read the full PDF: it evaluates Claude Opus 4.5 (high) on LiveCodeBench (reports pass@1 82.73%, a different number from and not a substitute for the already-tracked 83.7% Vals.ai figure) and on HumanEval-X/MBPP/BigCodeBench, but only reports AUROC/correlation uncertainty-prediction metrics for those, never a raw accuracy score — not usable for the math/humaneval gaps.

(3) GitHub eval-harness run logs — `gh search code` across GitHub surfaced dozens of hits for "claude-opus-4-5" + humaneval/math, nearly all of which were scattered, mutually-contradictory hardcoded numbers in unrelated hobby projects (humaneval seen as 87.4/91.0/92.0/92.5/92.7/95.4/96.5%; math_500 seen as 78.0/85.0/85.0/91.0/93.6/95.2/95.8/96.0/97.3% — no two agree) with no verifiable methodology, so none were used. One hit was different in kind: github.com/neulab/pace, a real CMU Neubig-Lab research repo backing the arXiv PACE paper (2607.02032), with genuine committed lm-evaluation-harness JSON output. Its humaneval_chat/neulab__claude-opus-4-5-20251101 result (verified by both `gh api` and a raw.githubusercontent.com fetch, and cross-checked against 5 sibling models' results in the same run batch for plausibility) gives pass@1 = 99.39% on the real 164-problem OpenAI HumanEval set — added below. The same repo's raw_results/ directory has task folders for aime25, gpqa, mmlu, mmlu_cot, humaneval, humaneval_chat, livecodebench, mbpp, mbpp_chat, swebench, etc., but NO math/math500 folder at all, so it does not resolve the MATH gap.

(4) Other leaderboards — fetched livebench.ai directly; it does not evaluate the standard MATH-500 or HumanEval benchmarks (its own categories are AMPS_Hard/math_comp/olympiad for math and LCB_generation/coding_completion for code), so it cannot corroborate or refute either gap. Separately found two independently-scraped, GitHub-hosted Artificial Analysis data snapshots (JacobLinCool/GPTD:data/aa-snapshot-full.csv and vinitaacharya/llm_providers_analysis:llmdata.csv) whose rows for "Claude Opus 4.5 (Reasoning)"/"(Non-reasoning)" both show a blank/empty math_500 field — external corroboration that Artificial Analysis itself has no MATH-500 number for this model.

(5) Non-English sources — searched Japanese, Korean and Chinese queries for independent MATH-500 testing of Opus 4.5. Every hit that gave a specific number recycled the same handful of inconsistent figures (95.2%, 85%, different again per query) traceable back to AI-generated SEO content-farm pages (automatio.ai, aithinkerlab.com) rather than any genuine regional-lab independent test. Spot-checked two of these sites' raw HTML directly: automatio.ai claims MATH 95.2%/HumanEval 92%/AIME 87% for Opus 4.5, while aithinkerlab.com's Opus-4.5-vs-4.6 comparison table claims MATH 78.3%/HumanEval 90.2%/SWE-Bench 51.4% for the same model — both contradict each other, and aithinkerlab's SWE-Bench figure (51.4%) directly contradicts this file's already-verified real SWE-bench-Verified score (80.9%, matching swe-bench-pro's 51.6% instead), confirming both sites fabricate or mislabel benchmark numbers. Neither was used as a citation.

(6) Sibling models — re-fetched the official Claude Opus 4.6 (anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-6) and Opus 4.7 (anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-7) launch pages directly and grepped for "math"/"humaneval": zero occurrences in either, same as Opus 4.5's own launch page — confirms the generational cutoff extends across the whole family's official materials. Additionally found github.com/isingmodel/evolution_of_benchmarks_in_frontier_models, an academic-style benchmark-citation tracking dataset, whose per-launch-post benchmark list for "Claude 4.5 (Opus)" includes SWE-bench Verified and Terminal-Bench 2.0 but not MATH-500 — while its entry for the earlier Claude 3.7 Sonnet launch does cite MATH-500 — independent external corroboration that Anthropic stopped citing MATH-500 starting with the Claude 4 generation, consistent with this file's 2026-07-16 finding.

Net outcome: 'humaneval' gap closed with a credible, independent, citable GitHub eval-harness score (99.39%, github.com/neulab/pace, tied to arXiv:2607.02032). 'math' (MATH-500) gap remains genuinely unresolved after exhausting all 6 prescribed new source types plus AA-snapshot cross-checks; re-confirmed as omitted_no_evidence — no vendor, independent evaluator, academic paper, or eval-harness repo found anywhere that has actually run Claude Opus 4.5 against MATH-500.
